title: IIS Web Site
description: Schema for an IIS web site resource as exposed by the Microsoft IIS Administration API. Web sites are a core entity of IIS that determine where and how HTTP requests will be handled, including binding configuration, application pool assignment, and resource limits.

name.description: The display name of the web site.

id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the web site by the IIS Administration API.

physical_path.description: The physical file system path for the web site root directory. Environment variables such as %SystemDrive% are supported.

key.description: The IIS site key identifier, corresponding to the site ID in IIS configuration.

status.description: The current operational status of the web site.

server_auto_start.description: Whether the web site starts automatically when the IIS service starts.

enabled_protocols.description: A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this web site.

limits.description: Resource limits governing connection behavior and URL processing for the web site.

connection_timeout.description: The time in seconds after which an inactive connection is closed.

max_bandwidth.description: The maximum bandwidth in bytes per second allowed for the web site. A value of 4294967295 indicates no limit.

max_connections.description: The maximum number of concurrent connections allowed for the web site. A value of 4294967295 indicates no limit.

max_url_segments.description: The maximum number of segments allowed in a URL path.

bindings.description: The bindings that define the protocols, IP addresses, ports, and hostnames the web site listens on. At minimum, a binding must specify a protocol, IP address, and port.

protocol.description: The network protocol for this binding.

binding_information.description: Combined binding string in the format '{ip_address}:{port}:{hostname}' for HTTP/HTTPS protocols.

ip_address.description: The IP address the binding listens on. Use '*' to listen on all available addresses.

port.description: The TCP port number the binding listens on.

hostname.description: The hostname for host header-based routing. An empty string matches all hostnames.

certificate.description: The SSL/TLS certificate associated with an HTTPS binding. Required when protocol is HTTPS.

name.description: The friendly name of the certificate.

id.description: The unique identifier of the certificate resource in the IIS Administration API.

issued_by.description: The distinguished name of the certificate issuer.

subject.description: The distinguished name of the certificate subject.

thumbprint.description: The SHA-1 thumbprint of the certificate.

valid_to.description: The expiration date and time of the certificate in ISO 8601 format.

require_sni.description: Whether Server Name Indication (SNI) is required for this HTTPS binding.

application_pool.description: The application pool assigned to handle requests for this web site.

name.description: The name of the application pool.

id.description: The unique identifier of the application pool.

status.description: The current operational status of the application pool.

_links.description: HAL-style hypermedia links to related resources.

halLink.description: A HAL-style hyperlink to a related resource.

href.description: The URI of the linked resource.
